strengths: Deep distributed-systems expertise with proven real-time production work at scale (Dunnhumby: millions of requests/second, microservices, low-latency inference)
Current hands-on experience with agentic frameworks, AI infrastructure, and MCP code generation at re:cinq demonstrating recent technical currency
15+ years of full-stack engineering with consistent track record shipping production systems across diverse tech stacks and industries
concerns: No demonstrated executive or leadership experience—current title is Staff Engineer, not CTO/VP Eng; job requires 5+ years leading engineering organizations at scale
Critical gap in fintech domain knowledge; RIDE Capital tenure (16 months) is vague and lacks detail on credit, trading, or compliance infrastructure
No evidence of scaling engineering teams from 10–30+ people or developing internal technical leadership layer, both core CTO responsibilities
response.justification: Szabo demonstrates strong technical fundamentals and recent exposure to agentic AI infrastructure, but the resume reveals critical gaps for a CTO role at a fintech agentic-AI company. He lacks demonstrated executive experience, fintech domain expertise, and evidence of scaling engineering teams—all hard requirements. His strength in distributed systems and current agentic work suggest technical capability, but his profile reads as a senior IC rather than an engineering leader responsible for organizational strategy, culture, and governance. The fintech gap is particularly acute given the role's emphasis on credit-system architecture and multi-jurisdiction regulatory compliance.
